content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agent design around a hierarchical 'episodic curiosity memory' architecture, where intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM/RND mechanisms are used to selectively prune and prioritize which exploration trajectories get stored into the agent's long-term active learning memory, rather than storing all experienced data uniformly. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent's past curious exploration directly shapes what new knowledge it will retain to ask better exploratory questions in future active learning rounds.
